---
title: "AI + LEA"
---

#### Parcours LEA - Affaires Economiques Internationales - UT2 - Jean Jaurès  
#### [Pierre-Alexandre Balland](https://www.paballand.com)  

### Start Here (Week 1–2)
Learn to use AI tools really well - most people use them badly, which means you can stand out just by being skilled.

ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini - learn prompting techniques (this is called prompt engineering)
Microsoft Copilot - deeply integrated in Excel, Word, PowerPoint, which are your daily business tools
Free resource: "Prompt Engineering for Everyone" on Coursera — no coding needed

Build Business-Specific AI Skills
These are what employers in business actually care about:

AI for data analysis — tools like ChatGPT + Excel or Julius AI let you analyze data without coding
AI for marketing — content generation, campaign ideation, customer segmentation
Automation without code — Zapier and Make.com let you automate workflows using AI, no coding needed
AI presentation & reporting — Gamma.app, Canva AI, Notion AI

Get a Credential Employers Recognise

Microsoft AI Skills (free, highly respected in business)
Google AI Essentials on Coursera (~10 hours, free to audit)
LinkedIn Learning AI courses — short, business-focused, and shows up on your LinkedIn profile

Make It Visible on Your CV

Add an "AI Tools" section to your CV listing the tools you're proficient in
Do a small project — e.g. use AI to analyse a business case, build a mock marketing strategy, or automate a process, and write it up as a case study
Join your university's AI or entrepreneurship society if one exists

The Mindset Shift That Matters Most
Employers right now aren't just hiring people who know AI — they're hiring people who can think about where AI fits in a business process. That's a business skill, not a tech skill, and you're already studying it.
